name: For Sale Pub
description: This Budapest bar allows drinkers to leave their mark on the walls, floor, and even ceiling.

name: Panoptikum Budapest
description: A series of caves under Castle Hill that once held the prisoner Vlad Tepes, better known as Count Dracula.

name: Szabo Ervin Library
description: A 19th-century artistocrat's mansion, turned into a library, hidden in a modern library.

name: Shoes on the Danube Promenade
description: A trail of iron footwear stands as a monument to the thousands executed along this riverbank during WWII.

name: Budapest Castle Hill Funicular
description: Built in 1870 at the bridge that connected Buda and Pest, this spectacular incline railway was almost lost forever during World War II.

name: Vajdahunyad Castle
description: This Hungarian castle was built out of cardboard and wood until it proved so popular that it got upgraded to stone.

name: Budapest Cave Church
description: Once the home of a hermetic monk, this cave now houses a small church.

name: Szimpla Kert
description: A Trabant car, a kangaroo statue, and a plethora of plants take over this abandoned-factory-turned-bar.

name: Red Ruin
description: This off-beat, quirky communism-themed bar isn't afraid to poke fun at its history.

name: Terror Háza
description: Museum dedicated to the terror regimes of Hungary.

name: The Citadella
description: From Austrians to Nazis to Soviets, this historic Budapest fortress has a history as complex as the city itself.

name: Epreskert Garden
description: The romantic garden was the epicenter of the Hungarian art scene in the 1880s.

name: Flipper Muzeum
description: Sitting beneath an unremarkable residential building is a museum devoted to classic Pinball.

name: Central Café and Restaurant 1887
description: Some of the greatest minds in the history of Hungarian art and science converged in this café.

name: Semmelweis Medical Museum
description: A museum in the childhood house of a medical pioneer.

name: The Golden Eagle Pharmacy Museum
description: A Hungarian pharmacy museum with a box full of mummy powder.

name: Budapest Chairlift (Zugligeti Libegő)
description: A  two-way chairlift system between Zugliget and the highest point in the city.

name: The Garden of Philosophers
description: Tucked away near more famous landmarks lies a statue meant to symbolize the confluence and continuing development of human culture.

name: Memento Park
description: Budapest's Communist-era statues, banished to the suburbs.

name: The Holy Right
description: The mummified right fist of a 1,000-year-old saint-king sits inside an ornate golden reliquary.

name: Columbo Statue
description: Uh, pardon me, ma'am, but I couldn't help noticing that there's a Columbo statue in the middle of Budapest.

name: Béla Lugosi Bust at Vajdahunyad Castle
description: Late at night, the bust of the "Dracula" actor was snuck onto the facade of this Budapest castle.

name: Hospital in the Rock
description: A museum dedicated to a secret military hospital hidden beneath a castle in Budapest.

name: Gyermekvasút
description: This quaint Hungarian railway system is operated exclusively by children between the ages of 10 and 14.

name: The Hungarian Geological Institute Building
description: This hidden art nouveau gem was designed by an architect in search of a national style.

name: Cinkota Old Cemetery
description: A forgotten graveyard lost in the tangles of an overgrown garden.

name: Budapest Agriculture Museum
description: A fake Transylvanian castle houses a cathedral of antlers.

name: Kerepesi Cemetery
description: Time stands still at this serene burial ground in the center of Budapest.

name: Péter Mansfeld Monument
description: This statue of a falling man commemorates a teenage martyr of the Hungarian Revolution.

name: Thermae Maiores
description: Legions of Roman soldiers bathed in this ancient spa complex under a Budapest transit station.

name: Michael Jackson Memorial Tree
description: A random tree outside a Budapest hotel has become a DIY shrine to the late singer.

name: Elektrotechnikai Múzeum
description: Electrical curiosity museum housed in an old transformer station.

name: Elektrotechnikai Múzeum
description: Electrical curiosity museum housed in an old transformer station.

name: Zwack Unicum Museum
description: Central Europe's largest collection of mini-bottles is on display at this museum devoted to a bitter Hungarian liquor.

name: Mihály Kolodko's Mini Statues
description: From cartoon characters to tiny tanks, a guerilla sculptor's little bronze statues are hidden throughout the city of Budapest.

name: Prince of Buda and Princess of Pest Statue
description: On Gellért Hill, this statue depicts the tragic tale of lovers divided by the Danube.

name: Bokod Floating Houses
description: This Hungarian stilt village is built in a lake that never freezes over due to the power plant that that uses it as a cooling pond.

name: Koller Gallery
description: The oldest private art gallery in all of Hungary.

name: Adria Palace
description: This building has survived for more than a century and remains virtually untouched.

name: Rosenstein
description: Dine on Hungarian-Jewish dishes at this multigenerational, family-run institution.

name: Holocaust Tree of Life Memorial
description: The metal leaves on this tree are engraved with the names of Holocaust victims.

name: The Flying Nun
description: The true meaning behind this sculpture remains unknown.

name: Mummies of Vác, Hungary
description: Hungarian mummies in beautiful painted coffins.

name: György Ráth Museum
description: A Hungarian optometrist's charming collection of Asian art and curiosities.

name: Ronald Reagan Statue in Budapest
description: A statue in Budapest's Liberty Square honors the former U.S. president's efforts to end the Cold War.

name: Zero Kilometer Stone
description: The geographical center of Budapest is marked with a 10-foot-tall stylized zero.

name: Hajmaskér Barracks
description: One of the largest barracks of the Austro-Hungarian monarchy's military is now an abandoned ruin.

name: Mátyás Fountain
description: A triumphant marble and bronze fountain depicting a 15th-century Hungarian king and his hunting party.

name: Soviet Ghost Town
description: The abandoned military base looks like a post-apocalyptic wasteland.

name: Tengerszem Nature Preserve
description: An industrial site turned into Hungary's most cherished natural wonder.

name: Salt Hill
description: The massive white mound and accompanying water terraces are a striking geological oddity.

name: Bullet Hole Markers at the Ministry of Agriculture Building
description: This easily overlooked memorial immortalizes the scars from the 1956 Hungarian Revolution.

name: Franciscan Church Ruin
description: A 13th-century convent where St. Margaret of Hungary spent her teenage years.

name: Steve Jobs Memorial Statue
description: The world’s first statue of the late Apple co-founder.

name: Ghetto Wall Memorial
description: The holes in this wall provide a glimpse into Jewish life in Budapest, both past and present.

name: Gorsium Archaeological Park
description: The remains of an entire Roman city can be explored in this interactive museum.

name: Magyar Rádió
description: This quiet, unassuming spot in Budapest was the birthplace of the bloody 1956 Hungarian Revolution.

name: Bory Castle
description: Hungarian self-built castle.

name: Busójárás
description: Festival of Perpetual Life and Rebirth.

name: Jewish Ghetto Wall
description: Hungarian Jews were ghettoized during the Second World War, but little remains of the original wall that closed the Jewish people off from the rest of the city.

name: ‘Kiskirálylány’ (‘Little Princess’)
description: A diminutive statue that was inspired by the sculptor's own daughter.

name: Miskolctapolca Cave Bath
description: The only natural cave bath in all of Europe.

name: Uránia Nemzeti Filmszínház
description: A stunning Art Nouveau cinema in the heart of Budapest.

name: Fekete Sas Patikamúzeum (Black Eagle Pharmacy Museum)
description: 300 years of pharmaceutical history on display.

name: Carl Lutz Memorial
description: This sculpture honors the man who was known as the "Righteous Among the Nations."

name: ‘The Fat Policeman’
description: Also known as Uncle Karl, the beloved bronze copper keeps a watchful eye on St. Stephen’s Basilica while people rub his lucky belly.

name: Gömböc
description: The largest statue of this convex, homogeneous object with just one stable and one unstable equilibrium point.

name: Szatmárcseke Calvinist Cemetery
description: Hundreds of boat-shaped headstones fill this graveyard as part of a centuries-old custom with mysterious origins.

name: Paul Street Boys Monument
description: A scene from one of Hungary's most famous novels.

name: Empress Sissi Statue
description: This memorial to a murdered empress took 20 years to design, then spent 30 years hidden away in storage.

name: Little Nemecsek Statue
description: This statue honors one of Budapest's most beloved literary characters.

name: Rubik R-26 Góbé
description: A giant glider—designed by the father of the Rubik's Cube's designer—now soars within a shopping mall.

name: Diósgyőr Castle
description: This castle played host to the largest great hall found in medieval Central Europe.

name: Righteous Among the Nations Memorial
description: A plaque bearing the names of those who dedicated themselves to protecting Budapest's Jewish population during World War II.

name: William Shakespeare Statue in Budapest
description: How did an Australian statue of the Bard of Avon end up in the Hungarian capital?

name: Library of the Sárospatak Reformed College
description: One of Hungary's most influential places of learning is also home to one of the world's most beautiful libraries.

name: Zalaszántó Stupa
description: One of the biggest Buddhist stupas in Europe, hidden from most locals.

name: Statue of Imre Nagy
description: A much-loved sculpture depicting the hero of a 1956 anti-Soviet uprising.

name: Harangház Bell House
description: A beautiful display of nearly 50 bells crafted with a unique design and distinct sound.

name: Taródi Vár
description: "Medieval" castle built in the mid-20th century by one family.

name: Szamos Marzipan Exhibition and Workshop
description: Everything from miniature biblical scenes to life-sized statues are almond-based at this sweet and nutty gallery.

name: The Basalt Organ
description: Take a hike to this unique rock formation formed by lava flows.

name: Memorial for Victims of the German Occupation
description: A highly-criticized monument in Budapest has become the site of a makeshift Holocaust memorial.

name: Esztergomi szénrakodó
description: An abandoned cable car station located on the Danube stands like a haunted house above the river.

name: Satoshi Nakamoto Bust
description: A tribute to the builder(s) of Bitcoin.

name: Matyó Museum
description: An exploration of a small ethnic group’s heritage through their exquisite folk art.

name: Széchenyi-Wenckheim Castle Ruins
description: The ruins of an early 1900s neo-Baroque mansion sit nestled in a local park in the Hungarian plains.

name: Recsk National Memorial Park
description: For decades, Hungary's Communist Party insisted this gulag had never existed.

name: Anna Kéthly Statue
description: A bronze woman in a stylized dress honors a courageous politician.

name: Premontre Monastery Church of Zsámbék
description: The remains of a 13th-century Romanesque church lie abandoned on a hillside.

name: Wenckheim-kastély
description: This castle was designed to have as many windows as there are days in the year.

name: Eger Castle Replica Ruins
description: Outside of Budapest, a reconstruction of a 16th-century fortress.

name: Zemplén Archives
description: These 13th century documents come housed in 18th century shelves organized by a 19th century historian.

name: Kopcsik Marcipánia
description: See more than 150 sculptures, stained glass windows, and famous works of art all rendered in marzipan.

name: Gül Baba's Tomb
description: This tomb of a Sufi mystic is a remnant of Ottoman rule in Hungary.

name: Lakihegy Radio Tower
description: World's tallest cigar-shaped radio tower.

name: Bud Spencer Statue
description: The bronze creation commemorates the life and work of a beloved actor.

name: House of Music Hungary
description: A controversial “crumpet”-shaped museum tries its best to blend in with surrounding parkland.

name: Wandering Apostles
description: Twelve towering statues once destined for Pécs Cathedral, now sheltering under arches.

name: Kindergarten Museum Martonvásár
description: This museum showcases the lives of Hungarian school children and their vintage toys.

name: RepTár Szolnok Aviation Museum
description: A museum of Hungarian military aviation in the middle of a small city.

name: The Pan-European Picnic
description: On this spot in 1989, nearly a thousand people broke through the Iron Curtain.

name: Hungarian Museum of Commerce and Hospitality
description: One of the few of its type in the world, this museum showcases hotels and business.

name: Istvántelek Train Yard
description: The abandoned "Red Star Train Graveyard" is the final rusting place for precious artifacts of train history.

name: Timewheel
description: Possibly the world's largest hourglass only needs to be reset every New Year's Eve.

name: Kelenföld Power Station
description: A marvel of technology a century ago, this disused power station now offers a lovely glimpse into the industrial design of the past.

name: Budapest Telephone Museum
description: Beautiful antiques and obscure engineering artifacts trace the evolution of phones from rare luxuries to ubiquitous.

name: Air Defense Early Warning Listening Ears
description: A relic of World War I that now exist as a piece of public art.

name: Katalin Karikó Mural
description: The Hungarian biochemist whose research helped develop mRNA-based vaccines for Covid-19.

name: Géró Udvar
description: The home of a deceased Hungarian artist is now on display for visitors.
